schema_note.normalization_note: confidence is copied from the real runtime skill; source_chain_refs are traced to the source chains; all other fields are normalized for paper presentation from the raw retrieved skill snippets.

normalized_skill_objects.name: Use Catalan-looking roadside or storefront text to narrow to Andorra
normalized_skill_objects.trigger_condition: image contains readable street-sign or storefront text in a compact Pyrenean settlement
normalized_skill_objects.raw_runtime_snippet: The signage is not common in Spain and France and the plate is not a typical European license plate with the blue strip on the left, leading to Andorra being the most likely

normalized_skill_objects.name: Use compact Pyrenean valley settlement layout to support an Andorra hypothesis
normalized_skill_objects.trigger_condition: image shows a compact mountain town embedded in a steep forested valley
normalized_skill_objects.raw_runtime_snippet: The compact urban layout fits Andorra la Vella, the capital city, which is set within tight Pyrenean valleys.

normalized_skill_objects.name: Use Pyrenean stone-and-brick mountain architecture to disambiguate Andorra-like scenes
normalized_skill_objects.trigger_condition: image shows grey stone or brick buildings in a mountainous Southern European town
normalized_skill_objects.raw_runtime_snippet: This location is particularly distinct due to the exposed rocky mountain in the background and the abundance of grey brick architecture, commonly found in the countries surrounding the Pyrenees like Northern Spain, Andorra, and Southern France

normalized_skill_objects.name: Use Thai script on roadside elements to identify Thailand
normalized_skill_objects.trigger_condition: image contains readable script on a traffic bollard, sign, or median marker
normalized_skill_objects.raw_runtime_snippet: The road signage uses what appears to be Thai script, visible on the center traffic bollard.

normalized_skill_objects.name: Use black-and-white roadside bollards to support Thailand over nearby alternatives
normalized_skill_objects.trigger_condition: image shows roadside bollards or posts with black-and-white banding
normalized_skill_objects.raw_runtime_snippet: The black and white bollard (street post) on the side of the road is also common in Southeast Asian countries but is most common in Thailand

normalized_skill_objects.name: Use broad east-west divided-highway cues to refine toward the Khon Kaen and Highway 12 corridor
normalized_skill_objects.trigger_condition: image shows a broad divided peri-urban highway with an east-west corridor feel in Thailand
normalized_skill_objects.raw_runtime_snippet: Based on these pieces of evidence, the most likely location is a rural area near Khon Kaen, Thailand.

normalized_skill_objects.name: Use Australian-style roadside reflector bollards to jump to inland Australia
normalized_skill_objects.trigger_condition: image shows roadside reflector posts in an open dry landscape
normalized_skill_objects.raw_runtime_snippet: The bollards (street posts with reflectors) on the side of the road are unique to Australia, but possibly found in countries in Europe but this climate can never be Europe

normalized_skill_objects.name: Use fan-shaped roadside weeds to localize toward Terengganu in Malaysia
normalized_skill_objects.trigger_condition: image shows fan-shaped roadside weeds in a humid left-driving tropical scene
normalized_skill_objects.raw_runtime_snippet: The weeds on the side of the road (that have a fan-like shape) are commonly found near and in the state of Terrangnu

normalized_skill_objects.name: Use American-style roadside mailboxes to support a US suburban prior
normalized_skill_objects.trigger_condition: image shows standalone roadside mailboxes in low-density residential context
normalized_skill_objects.raw_runtime_snippet: Mail boxes on the side of the road appear to be the American kind
